title: How I Actually Get to School Every Day

sentences.en: Someone at school asked me today how I get to school every morning, and my honest answer took way longer to explain than I expected.
sentences.zh: 今天有人問我每天早上怎麼去學校，結果我老實回答花的時間比我預期的還要久。

sentences.en: First, I ride my bicycle from my house to the bus station, which takes about five minutes if I pedal fast enough.
sentences.zh: 首先，我從家裡騎腳踏車到公車站，如果騎快一點大概五分鐘就到了。

sentences.en: Then I take the bus, which drives through three busy streets before it reaches a small bridge near the river.
sentences.zh: 接著我搭公車，公車會經過三條熱鬧的街道，才會到達河邊一座小橋附近。

sentences.en: On rainy days, the traffic on that road gets so bad that the bus barely moves at all, and I end up staring out the window for twenty minutes.
sentences.zh: 下雨天那條路的交通會塞到公車幾乎動不了，我常常盯著窗外發呆二十分鐘。

sentences.en: William takes a completely different way to his own school: Dad drives him in the car, since it is much closer to our house.
sentences.zh: William 去他自己學校的方式完全不同：爸爸開車載他去，因為他學校離我們家近很多。

sentences.en: William insists the car ride is boring, and he keeps asking Dad if they can take a truck instead, just for fun.
sentences.zh: William 堅持說開車很無聊，一直問爸爸能不能改開卡車，純粹好玩。

sentences.en: Bernice lives near the airport, so she sometimes sees an actual airplane flying low overhead on her walk to the bus stop.
sentences.zh: Bernice 住在機場附近，所以她走去公車站的路上有時候會看到真正的飛機低空飛過。

sentences.en: She said the sound used to scare her when she was little, but now she does not even look up anymore.
sentences.zh: 她說小時候那個聲音會嚇到她，但現在她連抬頭看都懶得看了。

sentences.en: Once, our whole family took a boat across the river instead of the bridge, just to see what it felt like.
sentences.zh: 有一次，我們全家搭船過河，而不是走橋，純粹想體驗看看那是什麼感覺。

sentences.en: It was much slower than the bus, but William loved every second of it and asked if we could take the boat every single day.
sentences.zh: 船比公車慢很多，但 William 每一秒都超享受，還問能不能天天都搭船去上學。

sentences.en: Sophia said a boat ride to school every day would mean waking up two hours earlier, and that ended William's boat dream immediately.
sentences.zh: Sophia 說如果每天搭船上學就得早兩個小時起床，這句話馬上讓 William 對搭船的美夢破滅了。

sentences.en: On the way home from school, I usually buy a bus ticket with my own money, since Mom says walking to school builds character but I am not always convinced.
sentences.zh: 放學回家的路上，我通常會自己花錢買公車票，因為媽媽說走路上學能培養品格，但我不是每次都相信這句話。

sentences.en: Last week, our bus stopped suddenly at a small train station so we could let a group of tourists pass by with their bags.
sentences.zh: 上星期，我們的公車在一個小火車站前突然停下來，讓一群拖著行李的觀光客先過。

sentences.en: The driver was very patient about it, even though several students on the bus complained about being a few minutes late.
sentences.zh: 司機對這件事很有耐心，儘管車上好幾個學生都在抱怨因此遲到了幾分鐘。

sentences.en: In the end, I still think riding my bicycle to the bus station is my favorite part of the whole trip, since it feels like the only truly quiet minute of my day.
sentences.zh: 到最後，我還是覺得騎腳踏車去公車站是整趟路程裡我最喜歡的部分，因為那感覺是我一天中唯一真正安靜的一分鐘。

quiz.question: How does William get to his own school?
options: Dad drives him in the car.

quiz.question: What does Bernice sometimes see on her walk to the bus stop?
Why does Sophia's comment end William's dream of taking a boat to school every day?
options: It would mean waking up two hours earlier.
There is no boat near their house.
